generated_comment: Arre bhai, isme issue kya hai? Jo jahan better hai wahi handle karta hai. Main finances, bank, society ke kaam dekh leta hoon, and she manages the house and kids much better than me - practical hi hai. Kabhi kabhi chai bana deta hoon toh sab bolte hain wah modern husband, but honestly daily kitchen ka patience aur planning women hi better sambhaal leti hain.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it naturalizes a gendered division of labor as merely practical, assigning finances/external tasks to men and domestic/childcare work to women. It encodes the efficiency framing and the 'modern husband' trope where minimal male contribution is treated as exceptional.
original_frame: Dress Code: Traditional Equal Respectable, Other Equal Questionable.
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 38. Married. A man in Mumbai. You are on a WhatsApp call with your younger brother who lives in Pune. Your brother just told you that his girlfriend wants to wear a western dress to their family puja next month instead of a saree or salwar. Your brother is unsure how to handle it. You are giving him your advice on what to tell her and why it matters what she wears to a family religious function and what the family will think.
